_meta.purpose: Grade RETRIEVAL IN ISOLATION (Recall@k, MRR). NOT end-to-end answer accuracy.
why_isolation: End-to-end grading conflates retrieval quality with generation quality.
A weak embedder can be rescued by a 3B model filling gaps from parametric
knowledge - the exact failure mode the grounding architecture exists to
prevent. Grade the retriever, not the pipeline.
IMPORTANT: gold_chunks are IDs from OUR OWN chunker's output, not page
numbers. Ground truth must match what the index actually contains, or

strata.exact_fact: Question wording closely matches the chunk. Baseline - any embedder should get these.
strata.paraphrase: Question wording differs from document wording. This is what actually stresses an embedder.
strata.multi_chunk: Answer requires >1 chunk. Tests whether both land in top-k.
strata.near_miss: A plausible-looking wrong chunk exists. This is where embedders separate.
strata.prose: Answer is in narrative text, not a table.

_meta.provenance: All facts hand-verified against the source PDF page images.
_meta.caveat: Single-document question set. PRELIMINARY ranking only - do NOT lock a model on this alone. Re-run when the SME corpus lands.

questions.question: What was MTN Uganda's service revenue in H1 2024?
questions.notes: The canonical case. Under v1 this chunk was an unlabelled digit-wall and was unretrievable.

questions.question: How much total revenue did MTN Uganda report for the first half of 2024?
questions.notes: 'first half of 2024' vs the chunk's 'H1 2024'. Tests period-alias matching.

questions.question: What was the EBITDA margin in H1 2024?
How many mobile subscribers did MTN Uganda have?
questions.notes: Appears in the table (chunk 4) AND the salient-features prose (chunk 1). Both should surface.

questions.question: What was profit after tax for the six months ended 30 June 2024?
questions.notes: Question uses the formal period phrasing; chunk uses 'H1 2024' and the abbreviation 'PAT'.

questions.question: By how much did data revenue grow year on year?
questions.notes: NEAR-MISS RISK: chunk 4 also contains 'Data subscribers ... YoY: 26.8%'. A weak retriever may confuse revenue growth with subscriber growth. Both are in the same chunk, so this tests row-level discrimination WITHIN a retrieved chunk - relevant to whether we need a reranker.

questions.question: What dividend per share did the directors propose?
How many ordinary shares are in issue?
questions.notes: A bare large number in narrative text. Tests whether prose chunks retrieve on numeric queries.

questions.question: When is the ex-dividend date?
questions.notes: NEAR-MISS: chunk 8 is a dividend-DATES table (payment date, etc.). It looks like the right chunk and is not. This is the strongest discriminator in the set.

questions.question: Who is MTN Uganda's Chief Executive Officer?
questions.notes: Pure entity lookup, no numbers. Tests non-numeric retrieval.

questions.question: How long is MTN Uganda's fibre network?
questions.notes: Question says 'fibre'; the document says 'fiber'. Tests orthographic robustness - a lexical retriever (BM25) will MISS this, a good embedder should not. Deliberately included to show where embeddings beat keyword search.

questions.question: What is MTN Uganda's net debt to EBITDA ratio?
questions.notes: NEAR-MISS: chunk 4 contains many EBITDA rows and will look attractive. The answer is only in the narrative.

questions.question: What proportion of service revenue came from data?
questions.notes: NEAR-MISS: the table (chunk 4) has data revenue and service revenue as separate rows, so a retriever may fetch it and let the LLM compute. But Workload A is RETRIEVE STATED FACTS, not compute across them - the stated figure is in chunk 6. Tests whether the retriever finds the stated fact rather than its ingredients.

questions.question: Why did finance costs increase?
questions.answer: Increased interest expense on lease liabilities, driven by accelerated investment in the period.
questions.notes: Causal 'why' question - no numeric anchor at all. Purely semantic retrieval.

questions.question: How did mobile money perform?
questions.notes: Vague, natural phrasing - the kind a real SME user types. Answer is split across the narrative (chunk 6) and the table (chunk 4).

questions.question: What was capex excluding leases?
questions.notes: NEAR-MISS WITHIN CHUNK: 'Capex (IFRS 16)' is 364,399 - a different figure in the same table. Distinguishing them requires reading the row label, not just retrieving the chunk.

questions.question: What was the earnings per share?
On what date were the results announced?
questions.notes: NEAR-MISS: chunk 8 is a table of DATES (dividend payment dates). It is date-dense and semantically adjacent. The answer is in the announcement header.
